name: Air Jordan 1 Retro High OG
materials: Premium leather upper, rubber cupsole, encapsulated Nike Air heel unit
description: Tinker Hatfield and Peter Moore's 1985 silhouette, returned in OG construction. The high-cut leather Jordan 1 reads the way it always has — a basketball shoe that left the court and never came back.

name: Air Force 1 '07
materials: Tumbled leather upper, perforated toe, encapsulated Air sole
description: Bruce Kilgore's 1982 design, untouched and uncoloured. The Triple White Air Force 1 has outlived every trend it has been part of — a uniform piece for anyone who treats footwear as wardrobe rather than statement.

name: SB Dunk Low Pro
materials: Leather and suede overlays, padded tongue, vulcanised Zoom Air sole
description: The Dunk reframed for skateboarding by Nike SB — beefier tongue, Zoom Air insole, and the colour-blocking that turned each release into a dated artefact. Built for board feel, worn for everything else.

name: 1906A
materials: Mesh and synthetic upper, ABZORB and Stability Web cushioning
description: Pulled from New Balance's late-90s / early-2000s tech-runner archive and re-cut for 2024. The 1906A keeps the layered overlays and ABZORB platform of its predecessors, in a cleaner, more wearable colour story.

name: Yeezy Boost 350 V2
materials: Primeknit upper, full-length Boost midsole, translucent rubber outsole
description: The runner-meets-sock that closed out the 2010s sneaker boom. Primeknit upper, full-length Boost, and the SPLY-350 stripe that made it one of the most recognisable silhouettes of its decade.

name: GEL-NYC
materials: Mesh and synthetic upper, GEL cushioning, dual-density EVA midsole
description: ASICS' modern hybrid — a NYC Marathon-inspired silhouette stitched from GEL-NIMBUS 3 and GEL-MC PLUS V DNA. Off-white mesh, soft suede overlays, and the GEL pocket that turns a runner into an everyday piece.
